pattern: Run-length encoding both the target and each candidate reduces stretchiness to a per-group comparison: same letter, and the target group is either equal or at least three while being no smaller.
hints: Comparing letter by letter is awkward; compress each string into runs of (letter, count) and compare run lists instead.
Two strings can match only if their run sequences have the same letters in the same order and the same number of runs.
For each aligned run the target count must equal the word count, or the target count must be at least three and at least the word count.

edge_inputs.description: first statement example with one stretchy word
second statement example where all words stretch
a group of size two cannot be stretched to match
extending requires size three or more, so size two target rejects
different letters never match even with right counts
word longer in a run than the target fails

stress.description: random target and words over a tiny alphabet so runs frequently align
stress.note: small alphabet keeps letters matching so the count rules get exercised
